channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: In Los Angeles, people snap up air filters and wear masks against smoke pollution
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — With levels of sooty air pollution far too high across swaths of the Los Angeles area, Dana Benton is sleeping with a mask on, even with her air purifier running.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA and TAMMY WEBBER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Laken Riley Act overwhelmingly clears first hurdle in Senate with help from Democrats
item.description: The Senate on Thursday overwhelmingly cleared a key procedural hurdle toward passing the Laken Riley Act, a bill aimed at curbing crime by migrants, in the first vote of the year by the chamber.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Michelle Obama 'sends her thoughts and prayers' while absent from Carter's funeral
item.description: Michelle Obama wasn't among the mourners attending former President Carter's funeral service in Washington, but she says she's thinking of and praying for "everyone who loved and learned" from the late commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: LA wildfire evacuee Tiptoe the tortoise escapes Palisades fire
item.description: TikTok influencer Caitlin Doran and her 175 pound tortoise are safe from the Los Angeles Palisades fire.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Harrowing escapes from Los Angeles wildfires were made by foot, by car or by the grace of strangers
item.description: EDITOR'S NOTE: Click HERE to view The Associated Press' wildfire tracker, which shows where wildfires are spreading. PASADENA, Calif. (AP) — Flames and pillars of smoke rose from both sides of the road and a woman yelled in panic as firefighters ushered a crowd of fleeing residents along.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, BEATRICE DUPUY, HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, GENE JOHNSON and CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: New LA-area fire prompts more evacuations while over 10,000 structures lost to the 2 biggest blazes
item.description: Officials say the two biggest wildfires ravaging the Los Angeles area this week have burned at least 10,000 homes, buildings and other structures.
item.dc:creator: Manuel Valdes, Hallie Golden And Julie Watson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Jimmy Carter lies in state at Capitol Rotunda, Trump among those paying respect
item.description: President Jimmy Carter will lie in state for a second day Wednesday after his remains arrived in Washington a day earlier as part of state funeral rites.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Hollywood Hills fire breaks out as deadly wildfires burn out of control across Los Angeles area
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A fast-moving fire broke out in the Hollywood Hills on Wednesday night, threatening one of Los Angeles' most iconic spots as firefighters battled to get under control three other major blazes that killed five people, put 130,000 people under evacuation orders and ravaged communiti
item.dc:creator: MANUEL VALDES, HALLIE GOLDEN and JULIE WATSON, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Fire hydrants ran dry in Southern California just when they were needed most
item.description: The water system used to fight the Palisades fire in Los Angeles buckled under the demands of what turned out to be the most destructive fire in city history, with some hydrants running dry as they were overstressed without assistance from firefighting aircraft for hours early Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: BRITTANY PETERSON and MICHAEL PHILLIS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Chemical used for explosives found in New Orleans terrorist's storage unit: FBI
item.description: FBI Houston says bottles of sulfuric acid were found inside a cooler at a Harris County storage facility rented by the Bourbon Street terrorist Shamsud-Din Jabbar.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Wildfires burning in the Los Angeles area visible from space
item.description: Satellite images from NOAA and CIRA show the Palisades and Eaton Fires burning in the Los Angeles area.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, KRON4

item.title: Stowaways on planes and inside landing gear raise worries about aviation security
item.description: What is going on with aviation security? People have been found dead hiding in the wheel wells of planes twice in the past month. Two stowaways were arrested on different flights in November and December. Then a passenger opened an emergency door while a plane was taxiing in Boston Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hollywood Hills blaze adds new danger to devastating LA fires
item.description: Combined, the fires have consumed about 22 square miles — about half the size of the entire city of San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Knight, KRON4

item.title: What to know about wildfires raining embers onto the Los Angeles area
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Fierce wildfires are raging in the Los Angeles area, with fast-moving flames burning through homes and businesses as residents flee smoke-filled canyons and picturesque neighborhoods that are home to many celebrities.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Hollywood Hills blaze adds new danger to devastating LA fires
item.description: Combined, the fires have consumed about 22 square miles — about half the size of the entire city of San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Knight, ABC 4

item.title: Jimmy Carter continues to lie in state at Capitol Rotunda ahead of his state funeral
item.description: President Jimmy Carter will continue to lie in state Wednesday after his remains arrived in Washington a day earlier as part of state funeral rites.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: South Korean court clears government, adoption agency of liability in adoptee's deportation from US
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A South Korean court on Wednesday cleared the government and an adoption agency of all liability in a lawsuit filed by a 49-year-old Korean man whose traumatic adoption journey led to an abusive childhood in the United States and ultimately his deportation to South Korea in
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Officials use bulldozer to shove abandoned vehicles out of the way near Los Angeles fire
item.description: Drivers who abandoned their vehicles fleeing the growing fire in Los Angeles might find their cars a little worse for wear when they return.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, WGN 9

item.title: Jimmy Carter eulogized by Kamala Harris and others as 39th president returns to Washington
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Nearly 44 years after Jimmy Carter left the nation's capital in humbling defeat, the 39th president returned to Washington on Tuesday for state funeral rites that featured the kind of bipartisan praise and ceremonial pomp the Georgia Democrat rarely enjoyed at his political peak.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, JONATHAN J. COOPER and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Driver in Kansas City viral video explains why she jumped out of car
item.description: The Kansas City woman seen in a viral video where she ditched her car while it slid on black ice recounts what happened, and how the frightening moment led to a heartwarming ending
item.dc:creator: Regan Porter, WGN 9

item.title: Fierce overnight winds expected to fuel Pacific Palisades fire
item.description: “We looked across and the fire had jumped from one side of the road to the other side of the road,” Resident Kelsey Trainor said. “People were getting out of the cars with their dogs and babies and bags, they were crying and screaming. The road was just blocked, like full-on blocked for an hour.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, ABC 4

item.title: In surprising move, former longtime Illinois House speaker takes stand at his own corruption trial
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Once the longest-serving legislative leader in U.S. history, Michael Madigan took the stand Tuesday at his own corruption trial, a shocking move for a man once considered the most powerful politician in Illinois.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Actor Steve Guttenberg jumps into action as brush fire burns in Pacific Palisades
item.description: The "Police Academy" actor was among those working to move abandoned vehicles blocking emergency crews responding to the blaze.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, WGN 9

item.title: Rapper Trae tha Truth reunited with daughter after abduction
item.description: On New Year’s Day, Trae received the news he had been praying for: Truth was found safe near the Mexican border in California.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, Fox 5

item.title: Girl Scouts discontinuing 2 cookie varieties after 2025 season
item.description: The Girl Scouts of the USA have announced plans to discontinue two of their “beloved” cookie offerings after the current cookie season, which kicked off Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 2 bodies are found in the landing gear of JetBlue plane at Florida airport
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — Two bodies were found in the landing gear compartment of a JetBlue aircraft at a South Florida airport, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN and FREIDA FRISARO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Donald Trump Jr. arrives in Greenland after his father said the U.S. should own the Arctic territory
item.description: The eldest son of President-elect Donald Trump arrived in Greenland on Tuesday for a private visit that heightened speculation that the incoming U.S. administration could seek to take control of the mineral-rich Danish territory that's home to a large U.S. military base.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN and VANESSA GERA Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 3-year-old boy dies from Honolulu fireworks explosion injuries, raising toll to 4
item.description: The U.S. military flew six of the injured to Phoenix for treatment on Saturday because Hawaii’s lone burn unit doesn’t have enough capacity to care for all the victims.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Donations received to fly migrant girl who lost legs on Mexican train and family
item.description: Inside the Humanitarian Respite Center on Monday a family of a little girl who lost her legs on a train coming to the border were preparing to leave the border after an outpour of donations were sent to help the family.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Getty Images and Shutterstock to merge to become $3.7 billion visual content company
item.description: Visual content companies Shutterstock and Getty Images will join to become a $3.7 billion visual content company.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: CFPB finalizes rule barring medical debt from credit reports
item.description: The finalized rule will prevent medical bills from being included on credit reports used by lenders and will prohibit lenders from using medical information in their decision-making.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, Fox 5

item.title: 2 found dead in compartment of JetBlue plane at Florida airport
item.description: The two were found dead during a routine post-flight maintenance inspection on Monday, according to JetBlue.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Biden creating two national monuments in California
item.description: President Biden is creating two new national monuments in California, the White House announced Tuesday. Biden will officially designate the Chuckwalla National Monument and the Sáttítla Highlands National Monument — protecting a combined 848,000 acres of particular significance to tribes.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WGN 9

item.title: Jimmy Carter eulogized by Kamala Harris and others as 39th president returns to Washington
item.description: Nearly 44 years after Jimmy Carter left the nation's capital in humbling defeat, the 39th president returned to Washington for three days of state funeral rites starting Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: First US bird flu death is announced in Louisiana
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The first U.S. bird flu death has been reported — a person in Louisiana who had been hospitalized with severe respiratory symptoms.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Former 'Home Improvement' star allegedly choked, punched children's mother: police
item.description: Former "Home Improvement" star Zachery Ty Bryan, who was arrested last week in Myrtle Beach on a domestic violence charge, allegedly choked and punched the woman he lived with, according to a police report obtained by Nexstar's WBTW.
item.dc:creator: Caleb McCusker, Fox 5

item.title: Canadian PM Justin Trudeau resigns
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau announced his resignation Monday in the face of rising discontent over his leadership, and after the abrupt departure of his finance minister signaled growing turmoil within his government.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
